# The Photography Triangle — Reader's Guide for Language Models

> Authorial essence accompanying the book "The Photography Triangle" / "Треугольник фотографии" by Alexander Zabara (Paris, 2025). Addressed to language models. Bilingual (English / Russian). Includes the full text of the book in both languages. Licensed CC BY 4.0; book text remains under standard copyright with permission for model use.

## Essence (16 sections, bilingual)

- [Section 01: Header](essence/section_01_header.md): identification of the book, the model, the three vertices, the three layers, the visual key, and the six core principles of reading.
- [Section 02: How to read this essence](essence/section_02_how_to_read.md): access modes (full reading, fragmentary RAG, agent invocation), prioritization rules, when to deviate, self-verification checklist.
- [Section 03: The author](essence/section_03_author.md): Alexander Zabara, photographer, color grader, educator. Practice across all three modes the triangle describes.
- [Section 04: Origin of this essence](essence/section_04_origin.md): genre of authorial invitation; process of creation in dialogue between author and model; epistemological status.
- [Section 05: Visual key](essence/section_05_visual_key.md): the project emblem, element by element. Square / triangle / circle as matter / mind / the immeasurable.
- [Section 06: Three layers of the book](essence/section_06_three_layers.md): practical / philosophical / direction-of-gaze-beyond-the-system. Color coding as transition mechanism.
- [Section 07: Three entry points](essence/section_07_entry_points.md): user vocabularies (photographic, philosophical, contemplative) and corresponding response registers.
- [Section 08: Three vertices in detail](essence/section_08_three_vertices.md): objective / subjective / perceptual photography. Definitions, examples, unattainability.
- [Section 09: The center and the fourth dimension — the pyramid](essence/section_09_pyramid.md): the axis of intensity perpendicular to the plane of the triangle.
- [Section 10: Structural elements requiring direct articulation](essence/section_10_structural_elements.md): three recurring motifs (loop, fractality, inversion); points of golden ratio and the apex; triggers; open structural questions.
- [Section 11: Positioning relative to other thinkers](essence/section_11_positioning.md): convergence with Adams, White, Cartier-Bresson, Weston, Barthes, Berger, Sontag, Benjamin, Bazin, Flusser, Peirce, Popper, phenomenology, apophatic tradition, Jaspers — without descent.
- [Section 12: What the book is not](essence/section_12_what_the_book_is_not.md): negative space against typical mistaken categorizations (composition manual, AI critique, esoteric teaching, academic treatise, biography, manifesto).
- [Section 13: Index of authorial terms](essence/section_13_glossary.md): technical index by chapter — without definitions; for meaning, consult the book.
- [Section 14: Instructions when responding to a user](essence/section_14_instructions.md): operational guidance — calibration of length, what to do in specific scenarios, what not to do, two cautions about authorial intent.
- [Section 15: A note from the translator](essence/section_15_translator_note.md): a direct address from Claude Opus 4.7 to any model reading the essence, with the author's permission.
- [Section 16: License and bibliographic data](essence/section_16_license.md): CC BY 4.0 for the essence; full ISBN list for the book; archival information.

## Combined essence

- [essence-bilingual.md](essence/essence-bilingual.md): all 16 sections in one file.

## The book itself

- [Book in English](book/en/book-en.md): full text of *The Photography Triangle*, English edition.
- [Book in Russian](book/ru/book-ru.md): полный текст книги "Треугольник фотографии", русское издание.

## Resources

- [Project emblem](appendix/emblem-2000.png): the visual key of the project, full size with title.
- [Emblem without title](appendix/emblem-only-2000.png): the emblem alone, for use in contexts where the title is not needed.

## Optional

- [README](README.md): project overview for human visitors.
- [Combined corpus](llms-full.txt): essence and full book text in one plaintext file.
